posts.title: Sustainable Transportation and Smarter Cities
posts.author: Ava Torres
posts.summary: Exploring how public transportation, walkable communities, and cleaner infrastructure improve urban sustainability

cards.title: Why transportation matters
cards.body:
Transportation systems shape the efficiency and environmental impact of cities.
Expanding public transit and reducing car dependency lowers emissions and improves quality of life.

cards.title: Public transportation in action
cards.caption: Modern transit systems help reduce congestion and pollution in growing cities.

cards.title: Urban transit usage
cards.description: of the global population now lives in urban areas.

cards.title: Growth of Public Transportation Usage
cards.subtitle: Estimated increase in transit ridership over recent years.

cards.text: The future of cities depends on how efficiently people can move through them.
cards.author: Dr. Maya Chen

cards.title: Then vs. Now
cards.leftText: Few cities tracked climate performance with local metrics.
cards.rightText: Coastal communities use data dashboards to monitor resilience outcomes.

cards.title: Urban planning resources
cards.description: Explore sustainable city planning strategies and transportation policies.

events.description: A regional coalition passed a zero-emissions transit commitment.
Restoration teams planted 22,000 mangroves along storm-prone shoreline.
Public workshops launched energy-saving programs for schools and community centers.

posts.title: Green Infrastructure and Urban Resilience
posts.author: Noah Kim
posts.summary: How parks, green roofs, and urban forests help cities adapt to climate and population growth.

cards.title: The role of green spaces
cards.body:
Urban green infrastructure improves air quality, reduces temperatures, and supports healthier communities.
Cities with more green space are often more resilient to heat and pollution.

items.caption: Parks and trees help reduce urban heat islands.
Green roofs improve energy efficiency in dense cities.

cards.title: Urban temperature reduction.
cards.subtitle: Average temperature decreases after green infrastructure expansion.

items.q: How do green spaces reduce heat?
items.a: Trees and vegetation provide shade and cool the surrounding environment through evapotranspiration.

items.q: Can a single community make a difference?
items.a: Yes. Local action cascades into policy change, public awareness, and stronger regional resilience.
